lookup_context: Only Category I aircraft are involved
Distances can be determined by suitable landmarks
items.task: Choose the distance condition that permits the succeeding aircraft to begin takeoff roll.
items.question_text: For Category I same-runway departures using the daylight landmark alternative in 3-9-6, what airborne-distance minimum is required?

choices.text: At least 3,000 feet between the aircraft.
At least 2,500 feet from the runway threshold.
At least 4,500 feet between the aircraft.
No distance minimum once the preceding aircraft is airborne.

items.explanation: For this Category I same-runway departure case, the landmark-based airborne-distance minimum is 3,000 feet.

items.task: Select the required interval and when it is measured from.
items.question_text: For intersecting runways in 3-9-8, what interval applies when a Category I departure follows a Category E departure and projected flight paths will cross?

choices.text: 2 minutes from when the preceding aircraft began takeoff roll.
3 minutes from when the preceding aircraft became airborne.
2 minutes from when the preceding aircraft crossed the runway end.
No interval applies if both aircraft depart from different runways.

items.explanation: Category I behind Category E requires a 2-minute interval measured from when the preceding aircraft began takeoff roll when projected flight paths will cross.

items.task: Choose the required wake interval and measurement point.
items.question_text: For 3-9-9 converging runway operations, what interval applies to Category B through I aircraft departing behind Category A when projected flight paths will cross?

choices.text: 3 minutes measured from when the preceding aircraft began takeoff roll.
2 minutes measured from when the preceding aircraft began takeoff roll.
3 miles radar separation before takeoff clearance.
No wake interval because the runways do not physically intersect.

items.explanation: For Category B through I behind Category A in this converging-runway case, the listed interval is 3 minutes from the preceding aircraft's takeoff roll.

lookup_context: Preceding landed aircraft: Category I or II
items.task: Choose the threshold-distance alternative before the preceding aircraft is clear.
items.question_text: For a Category I arrival behind a landed Category I or II aircraft, what daylight landmark distance in feet from the landing threshold permits the preceding aircraft not yet to be clear?

choices.text: At least 3,000 feet from the landing threshold.
At least 2,500 feet from the landing threshold.
At least 4,500 feet from the landing threshold.
No threshold distance alternative exists.

items.explanation: For a Category I arrival behind a landed Category I or II aircraft, the daylight landmark minimum is 3,000 feet from the landing threshold.

lookup_context: Arrival will fly through airborne path of departure
items.task: Select the required time interval.
items.question_text: What minute interval applies when a Category B through I arrival will fly through the airborne path of a departing Category A aircraft unless radar separation is used?

choices.text: 3 minutes.
2 minutes.
1 minute.
No interval if the runways are crossing runways.

items.explanation: The listed interval for Category B through I behind Category A in that crossing-runway arrival-behind-departure case is 3 minutes unless radar separation is used.

lookup_context: Question: lowest authorized restriction above airport
items.task: Choose the minimum altitude restriction.
items.instruction: Apply the altitude-restricted low approach minimum.
items.question_text: For an altitude-restricted low approach under 3-10-10, what is the minimum altitude above the airport?

choices.text: No less than 500 feet above the airport.
No less than 200 feet above the airport.
No less than 1,000 feet above the airport.
Any altitude requested by the pilot.

items.explanation: The paragraph sets 500 feet above the airport as the minimum altitude restriction.

lookup_context: Operation: simultaneous helicopter or powered-lift landings or takeoffs
Courses to be flown do not conflict
items.task: Choose the minimum distance between landing or takeoff points.
items.instruction: Apply the simultaneous landing/takeoff distance minimum.
items.question_text: If courses do not conflict, what minimum distance in feet is required between simultaneous helicopter or powered-lift landing or takeoff points?

choices.text: At least 200 feet.
At least 500 feet.
At least 1,000 feet.
No minimum distance if the courses do not conflict.

items.explanation: The required minimum distance is at least 200 feet, and the courses also must not conflict.

items.task: Choose the landmark-based departure distance minimum.
items.instruction: Apply the sea lane departure separation minimum.
items.question_text: What distance in feet is required when a Category I aircraft departs behind a Category II aircraft on the same sea lane and landmarks determine distance?

choices.text: At least 3,000 feet between the aircraft.
At least 2,500 feet between the aircraft.
At least 4,500 feet between the aircraft.
Only airborne status is required.

items.explanation: For a Category I aircraft behind a Category II aircraft, the landmark-based departure minimum is 3,000 feet.

lookup_context: Preceding arrival: Category I or II has landed
items.task: Choose the distance from landing threshold required before the preceding aircraft is clear.
items.instruction: Apply the sea lane arrival separation minimum.
items.question_text: What daylight landmark distance in feet from the landing threshold applies to a Category II arrival behind a landed Category I or II aircraft?

choices.text: 2,500 feet.
3,000 feet.
4,500 feet.
No distance alternative exists.

items.explanation: For a Category II arrival behind a Category I or II arrival, the daylight landmark minimum is 2,500 feet from the landing threshold.

items.task: Choose the usable distance values and note the flight-check limitation exception.
items.instruction: Apply the ILS usable height/distance table.
items.question_text: What ILS usable distances apply at 4,500 feet above the transmitter unless current flight-check limits differ?

choices.text: Localizer 18 miles; glideslope 10 miles.
Localizer 10 miles; glideslope 18 miles.
Localizer and glideslope both 25 miles.
Use 40 miles for both because ILS is treated as an H-class NAVAID.

items.explanation: The ILS table gives 18 miles for localizer and 10 miles for glideslope at 4,500 feet above the transmitter, unless current flight-check limitations differ.

lookup_context: Both NAVAIDs are within 30 NM of an unpublished fix
items.task: Choose the minimum divergence angle.
items.question_text: What minimum divergence angle applies when both NAVAIDs are within 30 NM of an unpublished fix?

choices.text: 30 degrees.
15 degrees.
45 degrees.
No divergence angle minimum applies.

items.explanation: Within 30 NM of the unpublished fix, the minimum divergence angle is 30 degrees.

items.task: Choose the maximum distance between route-defining points.
items.instruction: Apply the random RNAV route point-spacing limit.
items.question_text: For nonradar separation on a random RNAV route under 4-4-1, what is the maximum distance between defining points?

choices.text: 500 miles.
260 miles.
130 miles.
No maximum applies to RNAV routes.

items.explanation: For nonradar separation on random RNAV routes, the maximum distance between defining points is 500 miles.

lookup_context: Question: maximum distance between fixes used to define the route
items.task: Choose the maximum fix spacing.
items.question_text: Under 4-4-3, at FL180 and above, what maximum distance may separate the fixes used to define the route?

choices.text: 260 miles.
500 miles.
130 miles.
No maximum applies above FL180.

items.explanation: At FL180 and above, the maximum distance between fixes is 260 miles.

items.task: Choose the vertical separation minimum.
items.question_text: At or above FL290 under 4-5-1, what vertical minimum applies between a non-RVSM aircraft and any other aircraft?

choices.text: 2,000 feet.
1,000 feet.
500 feet.
No extra vertical minimum if one aircraft is RVSM capable.

items.explanation: When a non-RVSM aircraft is involved at or above FL290, the vertical minimum is 2,000 feet.

items.task: Choose the lowest usable flight level.
items.instruction: Apply the lowest usable flight level rule.
items.question_text: What is the lowest usable flight level when the altimeter setting is 29.92 or higher?
items.explanation: FL180 remains usable when the altimeter setting is 29.92 or higher.

lookup_context: Navigation source: VOR, VORTAC, or TACAN
Clearance below MEA but not below MOCA
items.task: Choose the distance from the NAVAID within which the exception applies.
items.question_text: Under 4-5-6, within what distance of a VOR, VORTAC, or TACAN may an aircraft be cleared below the MEA but not below the MOCA?

choices.text: Within 22 miles of that NAVAID.
Within 40 miles of that NAVAID.
Within 130 miles of that NAVAID.
At any distance if radar identified.

items.explanation: The VOR/VORTAC/TACAN exception is limited to within 22 miles of the NAVAID.

lookup_context: Holding would be below 5,000 feet AGL
Inbound toward airport on or within 1 statute mile of localizer between OM/substitute fix and airport
items.task: Decide whether holding may be authorized in the protected area.
items.instruction: Apply the ILS critical-area holding restriction.
items.question_text: When the 4-6-8 weather trigger exists, may ATC authorize holding below 5,000 feet AGL inbound toward the airport on or within 1 statute mile of the localizer between the OM/substitute fix and the airport?

choices.text: No, do not authorize that holding.
Yes, if the aircraft is above the glideslope.
Yes, if the pilot accepts responsibility.
Yes, because the restriction applies only below 3,000 feet AGL.

items.explanation: That is the protected area and altitude restriction for ILS critical-area holding.

lookup_context: Method uses first two digits of the flight level
items.task: Choose the arithmetic rule for the descent point.
items.question_text: How is the descent point determined from a flight level to avoid excessive low-altitude flight?

choices.text: Add 10 to the first two digits of the flight level.
Subtract 10 from the first two digits of the flight level.
Use the last two digits of the flight level.
Use a fixed 20-mile descent point for all flight levels.

items.explanation: The paragraph states that the descent point is determined by adding 10 to the first two digits of the flight level.

items.source_label: 5-1-6 RNAV ATS route protected width note
items.task: Choose the route width and protected distance on each side of centerline.
items.instruction: Apply the RNAV route width note.
items.question_text: What protected width in miles applies to RNAV ATS routes?

choices.text: 8 miles total, with 4 miles on each side of route centerline.
4 miles total, with 2 miles on each side of route centerline.
10 miles total, with 5 miles on each side of route centerline.
No lateral protected width is specified.

items.explanation: The note states an 8-mile route width with 4 miles of protected airspace on each side of centerline.

lookup_context: Compare altitude readout to pilot-reported altitude
items.task: Choose the maximum permitted variation.
items.instruction: Apply the altitude readout validation tolerance.
items.question_text: When is an altitude readout valid compared with the pilot-reported altitude, using the feet-based tolerance?

choices.text: When it varies less than 300 feet.
When it varies less than 500 feet.
When it varies less than 1,000 feet.
Whenever the aircraft is radar identified.

items.explanation: The validation standard is variation less than 300 feet from the pilot-reported altitude.

lookup_context: Purpose: achieve or maintain required/desired spacing
items.task: Choose how aggressively speed adjustments should be used.
items.question_text: When achieving or maintaining required or desired spacing, what minimum-necessary limit applies to speed adjustments?

choices.text: Keep them to the minimum necessary.
Use the largest speed change available to solve spacing quickly.
Use speed adjustments only after issuing vectors.
Avoid speed adjustments whenever aircraft are on published routes.

items.explanation: Speed adjustments must be kept to the minimum necessary to achieve or maintain required or desired spacing.

items.source_label: 5-7-3 below Class B / VFR corridor speed limit
lookup_context: Aircraft is below Class B airspace or in a VFR corridor through Class B airspace
items.task: Choose the maximum assignable speed.
items.instruction: Apply the Class B related speed limit.
items.question_text: What maximum speed in knots may be assigned below Class B airspace or in a VFR corridor through Class B airspace?

choices.text: Not more than 200 knots.
Not more than 210 knots.
Not more than 250 knots.
No speed limit applies if ATC assigns the speed.

items.explanation: The speed limit in that situation is not more than 200 knots.

items.source_label: 5-8-4 departure from arrival on final minimum
lookup_context: Departing aircraft versus arriving aircraft on final
Separation will increase within 1 minute after takeoff
items.task: Choose the initial and increasing separation requirements.
items.instruction: Apply the departure/arrival radar separation minimum.
items.question_text: What minimum may separate a departure from an arrival on final if separation increases to 3 miles, or 5 miles when 40 miles or more from the antenna, within 1 minute after takeoff?

choices.text: 2 miles.
1 mile.
3 miles immediately with no increase requirement.
5 miles immediately in all cases.

items.explanation: Except as provided in 5-8-5, the rule allows 2 miles initially if separation increases to the required 3 or 5 miles within 1 minute after takeoff.

lookup_context: Altitudes must be at or above the published MDA
Applies for each mile on final
items.task: Choose what ATC must tell the pilot.
items.question_text: If recommended altitudes are requested, what must ATC tell the pilot about altitudes at or above the published MDA for each mile on final?

choices.text: That recommended altitudes will be given for each mile on final.
That only the final MDA will be given.
That recommended altitudes below MDA may be issued if requested.
That altitudes are unavailable during surveillance approaches.

items.explanation: If requested, tell the pilot that recommended altitudes at or above the published MDA will be given for each mile on final.

lookup_context: Question: display extent above highest and below lowest sector altitude/flight level
items.task: Choose the display-filter buffer.
items.question_text: When 1,000-foot vertical separation is applicable, how far above the highest and below the lowest sector altitude or flight level must the display include?

choices.text: 1,200 feet above and below.
1,000 feet above and below.
2,000 feet above and below.
2,200 feet above and below.

items.explanation: Where 1,000-foot vertical separation applies, the display includes 1,200 feet above the highest and below the lowest altitude or flight level of the sector.

items.task: Choose the default interval.
items.question_text: What default minimum interval applies between successive timed approaches unless a listed exception applies?

choices.text: 2 minutes or a 5-mile radar interval.
1 minute or a 3-mile radar interval.
3 minutes or a 10-mile radar interval.
No default interval if aircraft are established inbound.

items.explanation: The basic interval is 2 minutes or 5 miles unless one of the listed exceptions applies.
